title: ParseInt error with --device-cgroup-rule 'c *:* rwm'
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Unable to parse wildcard for major device number in device cgroup rule.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run --rm --device-cgroup-rule 'c *:* rwm' fedora cat /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/devices.list`


**Describe the results you received:**

```
root@ubuntu:~# podman run --rm --device-cgroup-rule 'c *:* rwm' fedora cat /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/devices.list
Error: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "*": invalid syntax
```

Even when passing a specific device major number the behaviour is different to docker (no cgroup device restrictions):
```
root@ubuntu:~# podman run --rm --device-cgroup-rule 'c 1:* rwm' fedora cat /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/devices.list
a *:* rwm
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

As per https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#device-cgroup-rule-type-major-minor-mode, should accept `*` as wildcard to allow all device major numbers (as well as minor numbers, which doesn't error).

Expected the same behaviour as docker, which is to output the following (second line modified from the default of `c *:* m`):
```
root@ubuntu:~# docker run --rm --device-cgroup-rule 'c *:* rwm' fedora cat /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/devices.list
b *:* m
c *:* rwm
c 1:3 rwm
c 1:5 rwm
c 1:7 rwm
c 1:8 rwm
c 1:9 rwm
c 5:0 rwm
c 5:1 rwm
c 5:2 rwm
c 10:200 rwm
c 136:* rwm
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes - ParseInt error still seen on podman v4.1.0 (although device restrictions are different on cgroups v2).

comments.body: @karthikelango137 Want to take a look at this?

I'm interested in what's considered the correct fix here. While there are no device cgroup restrictions applied by podman by default there is no use for the `--device-cgroup-rule` arg as far as I can tell, other than consistency with the docker CLI? Should there be the same cgroup restrictions as docker by default?

One side point is that 'device cgroup rule' seems an inaccurate name for the arg under cgroups v2 where there is no device cgroup controller? (However, again, it's reasonable for the main goal to be consistency with docker. Or perhaps it is/should be considered deprecated on cgroups v2?)
